findings.effort: Often one default setting in your export.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_accessible on every trip (most small-agency fleets are 100% accessible, so this is often a single default).
findings.what: 1 of 385 trips don't say whether the vehicle is wheelchair accessible.
findings.why: Even with accessible stops, riders need to know the bus itself can take them.

findings.effort: Worth it for multi-level or large stations; flat stops don't need it.
findings.fix: Add pathways.txt connecting entrances, platforms, and any elevators, with a level for each.
findings.what: This feed models stations or entrances but has no pathways.txt.
findings.why: Trip planners can't guide riders through the station, and there is no step-free route information for wheelchair users.

completeness.name: completeness
completeness.summary: 100% of stops state wheelchair accessibility (88% marked accessible, 12% marked not accessible). This measures what the feed publishes, not whether a stop is physically usable. Fare data is published.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'stop_too_far_from_shape_using_user_distance' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Stop too far from shape using user distance (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'trip_distance_exceeds_shape_distance_below_threshold' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Trip distance exceeds shape distance below threshold (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'route_long_name_contains_short_name' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Route long name contains short name (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'big_gap_in_service' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Big gap in service (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'unknown_file' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Unknown file (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

correctness.name: correctness
correctness.summary: The MobilityData validator flagged 5 kinds of issue across 10 instances (0 error, 5 warning, 5 informational).

freshness.name: freshness
freshness.summary: Service data covers the next 180 days.

findings.fix: Check the trip updates endpoint with your AVL vendor; it should return a fresh GTFS-Realtime protobuf on every request.
findings.what: The trip updates realtime feed failed during sampling.
findings.why: When this feed is down, riders see scheduled times presented as if they were live.

findings.fix: Check the vehicle positions endpoint with your AVL vendor; it should return a fresh GTFS-Realtime protobuf on every request.
findings.what: The vehicle positions realtime feed failed during sampling.
findings.why: When this feed is down, riders see scheduled times presented as if they were live.

findings.fix: Set the GTFS-Realtime FeedHeader.timestamp on every response.
findings.what: Realtime feeds didn't include a header timestamp, so freshness couldn't be checked.
findings.why: Without a header timestamp, apps and this scorecard can't tell how old the data is.

findings.fix: Check with your AVL vendor that every vehicle assignment flows into TripUpdates, including school-day and tripper runs.
findings.what: 1 of 1 trips scheduled during the sampling window had no live predictions.
findings.why: Riders on those trips get schedule data dressed up as realtime. Caltrans expects every operating trip in TripUpdates.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.summary: Sampled 3 times: 1 of 3 feeds healthy; 0.0% of scheduled trips had live predictions.

criteria.detail: Service data covers the next 187 days.
States wheelchair access on 100% of stops and 100% of trips.

conformance.summary: This feed earns the conformance mark: valid, current, and stating wheelchair access on nearly every stop and trip.

feed.license_note: No stated data license in the Mobility Database; verify before publishing.

ntd_id_alignment.detail: If you report to the National Transit Database, your GTFS agency_id should be your five-digit NTD ID by report year 2026 (2025 for full reporters). We don't have your NTD ID on file, so this one is not checked yet.

pillars.detail: Service data covers the next 187 days.

ntd_readiness.summary: This feed looks ready for your NTD certification: published at a public URL, valid, and current. The official check is still your D-10 certification.

recommendations.effort: A per-route color setting in your scheduling software.
recommendations.fix: Adjust route_color or route_text_color so each pair clears 4.5:1. Often switching the text between black and white is enough.
recommendations.what: 4 route badge(s) pair a color and text color below the WCAG 4.5:1 contrast bar (E Grn, E Prpl, E Red, A Red).
recommendations.why: Low-contrast route badges are hard to read for riders with low vision and wash out on a phone screen in sunlight at the stop.

recommendations.effort: Worth it for multi-level or large stations; flat stops don't need it.
recommendations.fix: Add pathways.txt connecting entrances, platforms, and elevators, and levels.txt for each floor, so the step-free route is described.
recommendations.what: This feed models stations or entrances but has no pathways.txt and levels.txt.
recommendations.why: Without pathways and levels, a wheelchair user can't tell whether a step-free route exists inside the station, and trip planners can't route anyone through it.

top_fixes.fix: Check the trip updates endpoint with your AVL vendor; it should return a fresh GTFS-Realtime protobuf on every request.
top_fixes.what: The trip updates realtime feed failed during sampling.
top_fixes.why: When this feed is down, riders see scheduled times presented as if they were live.

top_fixes.fix: Check the vehicle positions endpoint with your AVL vendor; it should return a fresh GTFS-Realtime protobuf on every request.
top_fixes.what: The vehicle positions realtime feed failed during sampling.
top_fixes.why: When this feed is down, riders see scheduled times presented as if they were live.

top_fixes.fix: Check with your AVL vendor that every vehicle assignment flows into TripUpdates, including school-day and tripper runs.
top_fixes.what: 1 of 1 trips scheduled during the sampling window had no live predictions.
top_fixes.why: Riders on those trips get schedule data dressed up as realtime. Caltrans expects every operating trip in TripUpdates.
